evidence_quotes.quote: Even privacy-conscious users are profiled indirectly.
Your personal data is their industry secret, secret they sell only to other big tech companies to avoid political backlash; they never show how much they know and they probably never will.

paragraphs.text: Hello, Let me briefly introduce myself and explain why I hope my feedback is taken seriously. You can call me An, an anonymous from Italy. Thanks to my older brothersone a rocket engineer, the other a graphic designerIve been active online since 1998, when I was about five. I wasnt just playing games: I was soon reading, searching, using eMule, torrents, IRC, modding, setting up private servers, and interacting across platforms well before mainstream adoption in Italy. Since my English was limited at the time, I used Spanish until I was 14, when my English became sufficient. When Bitcoin was around 23, I urged my family to buy itjust because darknet users complained about frozen accounts. Im not rich because I didnt want to sell my consoles for BTC, ahah! Ive seen firsthand most of the problems the Commission aims to addressdark patterns, addictive design, lack of enforcementbut I wasnt affected. Perhaps because of my familys education or early exposure to system mechanics. I dislike vague anecdotes and prefer datalike the replication crisis or simple empirical models. But in this format, personal testimony is the only meaningful input I can offer. Cultural prevention is key. One of the best ways to improve consumer behavior is to increase freedom in the creation and consumption of products: Hardware and software Media and tech And, most importantly, the cultures around them Key concerns: The drop in media quality is not accidental. A satisfied customer isnt always a returning one. Tech corporations benefit from cheap, isolated, mobile, hyper-specialized individuals free from family ties. They fund political polarization and anti-birth campaigns for this reason. Similar to the corporate encouragement of female labor post-WWII, big companies were not pro-feminismthey supported the cultural change for money. It's not evil, just profitable. Following these plans, game media companies push back against campaigns like Stop Killing Games because it goes against a subscription model with planned obsolescence. The core issue isnt ideologyits instrumentalization. Google can log nearly everythingfrom Android keystrokes to browser history. Even privacy-conscious users are profiled indirectly. Nothing to hide is not a defenseit misunderstands how informational power works. Were entering a subscription-based world where nothing is owned, and everything is controlled. From how many seconds you spend watching certain videos, and which one of them increased the average time spent on the platform, to spying on any bubble you interact with online and offline, and overall knowing all your relations and habits. You can safely assume everything is known to the second for most peopleor it can be known if a big tech company decides to know. Your personal data is their industry secret, secret they sell only to other big tech companies to avoid political backlash; they never show how much they know and they probably never will. The objectives are clear: Lower costs for specialized labor Maximize consumer dependency Isolation over socialization. Addiction over satisfaction. Without confronting this dynamic, reforms will remain superficial. Im not anti-capitalisthyper-capitalism lifted billions from povertybut todays digital economy undermines consumer autonomy. My recommendations: Protect repair rights (see Louis Rossmann) Extend Stop Killing Games initiatives to all software (see Ross Scott) Stop suppressing papers that found piracy did not affect sales Dont pressure individual EU countries to model themselves after Germany Legislate against excessive behavioral personalization that exploits psychological vulnerabilities Ultimately, even if big tech is morally right, this debate should center on consumers' self-interest. The Colosseum is for our escapismplease respect our individual freedom to enjoy it. Thank you for the opportunity to contribute.
